attacking_style: Launches solo attacks from large breakaways on final climbs, particularly effective on steep gradients (14-16% ramps). Capable of extreme-distance efforts, attacking as early as 70km out on categorized climbs to establish position.
team_tactics: Team places multiple riders (3-5) in early breakaways to enable energy conservation. Storer refuses pulls while teammates work, preserving strength for final climb attacks. DSM/Sunweb consistently executed this numerical advantage strategy.
terrain_preference: Steep final climbs from breakaway situations are optimal. Handles category 2 climbs with sustained gradients effectively. Shown competent descending ability, adding versatility beyond pure climbing. Struggles when forced to chase or position defensively in reduced groups.
vulnerability: Poor breakaway positioning instincts when defending jersey (2023 Vuelta mountains jersey loss). Ineffective when caught behind in chase groups rather than establishing early breaks. Requires team support for energy conservation; less effective in reactive racing.
key_patterns: Wins exclusively from breakaways, never from reduced peloton or GC group scenarios.
Team places 3-5 riders in breaks to control tempo and shield Storer (4 instances).
Attacks on final climb from established breaks, not bridge moves or counterattacks (5 wins/podiums).
Targets KOM points strategically before peloton response, planning intermediate collections methodically.
Refuses breakaway cooperation when teammates present, conserving energy for final effort (2 documented stages).
Most successful 2021 Vuelta with multiple stage wins; 2023 showed defensive tactical weaknesses.
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Vuelta showed tactical regression with poor positioning leading to mountains jersey loss despite starting as favorite. Peak form was 2021 Vuelta with multiple wins.
